question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Short answer - You have multiple, separate issues that look like different root causes — not a single “one-thing” outage. Some SharePoint problems match known identity / sensitivity-label / token-caching failure modes and typically require 2nd‑level (Identity/M365) work. The Teams device problems (camera missing; mic input zero) match simple OS/permission or hardware-connection causes and are usually solvable by 1st‑level support with the standard “Blueprint” checks

Cloud-native group 2. Synchronized On-Prem group Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated. SharePoint referenced the synchronized version. Actions 11.04.25 – 14.04.25 • Consolidation of group structure • Old Cloud group removed • On-Prem group properly cleaned • External guests managed as Cloud-only members • Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed 15.04.25 – Monitoring Access remains stable No further inconsistencies 17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 07 The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized). Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
